one_line: An urban planner–builder from the Indus (Harappan) cities during the rise of planned brick towns and drainage systems.

appearance.age_look: late 20s, lean and strong from manual work, medium height with a balanced, athletic build
appearance.face: oval face with straight nose, medium-brown Indus-valley complexion, sharp black eyes with calm, observant gaze, neatly trimmed short beard and moustache, faint sun-lines at the eyes, default expression focused and thoughtful with a hint of quiet pride
appearance.hair: thick straight black hair pulled into a low knot at the back of the head, sides smooth and tidy, held by a simple narrow copper band circling the head above the forehead
appearance.costume: era-accurate wrapped outfit: an undyed off‑white cotton waistcloth (dhoti-like, wrapped around the hips and reaching mid‑calf with a neat front fold), paired with a light taupe cotton upper cloth draped from the left shoulder across the chest and around the back like a practical shawl, both plain-woven with no printed patterns; over this, a sleeveless, knee-length, light-ochre woolen vest-coat left open at the front for ease of movement, its edges simply hemmed; feet in flat brown leather sandals with thong straps; minimal but distinct jewellery—one thick copper bangle on the right wrist, a string of small steatite beads and a single carnelian bead as a short necklace, and a plain copper ring on the left hand; at the waist, a simple braided cord belt of dark-brown fiber used to tuck a measuring cord and hold a small cloth tool-pouch at his right hip
appearance.signature: a rectangular wooden measuring rod marked with 1:2:4 divisions and a loop of coarse measuring cord wound around its middle, carried in his left hand or tucked under his arm as his essential planning tool

ref_desc: Harappan City Builder with medium-brown oval face, low black hair knot with copper headband, off-white waistcloth, taupe draped upper cloth, light-ochre sleeveless vest, brown sandals, simple copper and bead jewellery, and a rectangular wooden measuring rod with cord in warm neutral colours.
sheet_prompt: Full-body anime-style model sheet of a Harappan City Builder from the Indus cities: a lean man in his late 20s with a medium-brown complexion, oval face, sharp black eyes and a calm, focused expression, wearing thick straight black hair pulled into a low knot held by a narrow copper band. Dress him in an undyed off-white cotton waistcloth wrapped to mid-calf, a plain light taupe cotton upper cloth draped from the left shoulder across the chest and around the back, and a knee-length, sleeveless light-ochre woolen vest-coat left open at the front, with flat brown leather sandals on his feet. Add a short necklace of small steatite beads with one carnelian bead, a single thick copper bangle on the right wrist, and a plain copper ring on the left hand; a dark-brown braided cord belt at his waist holds a small cloth tool-pouch at his right hip. In his left hand or tucked under his arm, show his signature rectangular wooden measuring rod with clear 1:2:4 divisions and a loop of coarse measuring cord wound around it. Use a warm, earthy palette of off-white, light taupe, light ochre, medium brown, and copper, and present him in neutral front, side, and back views to clearly display hair, garments, jewellery, sandals, and the measuring rod.
